mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of rate-decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate breaches the 5.0 percent threshold, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% over a 60-day window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and real estate investment, producing positive equity returns as corporate earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS and HOUST move in opposite directions, or the interaction term Rank IC is negative over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of persistent inflation above target, rising CPIAUCSL values trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 70% of months where CPIAUCSL rises above the 0.4% threshold during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing its annual trend indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of months where INDPRO crosses above its 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate creates a dual confirmation of labor market tightening, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 55% of observation windows, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate profit fundamentals, which systematically translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth rates, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in retail sales growth signals immediate consumer pullback, which triggers negative short-term equity returns as earnings revision expectations turn downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth falls below 0.2 percent
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 65% of months where retail sales growth drops below the 0.2% threshold.
